back: Confidentiality (secrecy), Integrity (no unauthorized change), Availability (accessible when needed).
Categories: technical, managerial, operational, physical. Types: preventive, detective, corrective, deterrent, compensating, directive.
Never trust by location; verify every request explicitly and grant least privilege.

front: Phishing vs spear phishing vs whaling
back: Phishing: broad. Spear phishing: targeted at specific people. Whaling: targets executives.
Malware that encrypts files and demands payment for the key. Best defense: tested offline backups.
Well-resourced (often nation-state) actor seeking long-term, stealthy access for strategic goals.
An isolated segment for internet-facing services so their compromise doesn't expose the internal LAN.
Data at rest (storage), in transit (network), and in use (memory) — each needs its own protections.
Redundancy duplicates components; resilience is the broader ability to keep operating and recover from failure.
Something you know, something you have, something you are (plus somewhere you are / something you do).
Preparation → Identification → Containment → Eradication → Recovery → Lessons learned.
Centralizes and correlates logs from many sources to detect, alert on, and investigate incidents.
Accept, avoid, transfer (e.g., insurance), and mitigate (reduce likelihood or impact).
Policy = intent/rules; standard = specific requirements; procedure = step-by-step how-to.
RTO = target time to restore service. RPO = maximum acceptable data loss measured in time.
